item.title: Summer Culture Preview
item.description: What’s coming this season in TV, theatre, music, movies, dance, and art.

item.title: How Prepared Are We for a Public-Health Emergency?
item.description: The outbreaks of hantavirus and Ebola expose the shortsightedness of America’s retreat, under the Trump Administration, from its role as a global-health leader.
item.dc:creator: ByDhruv Khullar

item.title: Singing the Knicks’ Praises, with a Dash of Metal
item.description: Doug Berns, a.k.a. DugLust, is a Knicks nut and a modern-day Weird Al, who counts Ben Stiller among his devotees. With the team in the playoffs, he’s busy.
item.dc:creator: ByAmanda Petrusich

item.title: The Trump-Epstein Files: Look but Don’t Touch
item.description: The Donald J. Trump and Jeffrey Epstein Memorial Reading Room, in Tribeca, housed three and a half million bound files, along with a handy time line charting the ickiness.
item.dc:creator: ByCharlotte Goddu

item.title: Mariska Hargitay Trades Her Badge for Confetti
item.description: On a break from “Law & Order: S.V.U.,” the actress takes over for Daniel Radcliffe in “Every Brilliant Thing”—steps away from where her mother, Jayne Mansfield, had her Broadway début.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Larson

item.title: The Useless Beauty of Christo and Jeanne-Claude
item.description: Plans for a forgotten art work by the late duo were found in an airshaft. Now, with the help of Christo’s nephew, “Air Package on a Ceiling” is finally on view, at Gagosian.
item.dc:creator: ByAnna Russell

item.title: How Problematic Is Patriotism?
item.description: National pride in America has plummeted in the Trump era. Is it worth trying to salvage?
item.dc:creator: ByArthur Krystal

item.title: Our Warming Planet Is a Petri Dish for New and Deadly Microbes
item.description: “Flesh-eating” bacteria is spreading. Infectious fungi are emerging. Microbiomes may never be the same. Are we ready?
item.dc:creator: ByShayla Love

item.title: The Stunt Pilot Hunting Russian Drones
item.description: A Ukrainian flying ace is leveraging his aerobatics skills to protect his countrymen from nightly attacks.
item.dc:creator: ByEd Caesar

item.title: How “The Chosen” Spurred a Golden Age of Christian Filmmaking
item.description: Dallas Jenkins’s show—a prestige drama about Jesus’ life that became the biggest crowdfunded television project in history—has come to model the sort of bottom-up, fandom-first entertainment that is quietly reshaping the industry.
item.dc:creator: ByRachel Monroe

item.title: A Vindication of the Rights of L.L.M.s
item.description: Pity the poor large language model!
item.dc:creator: ByCora Frazier

item.title: “Many Worlds”
item.description: Wasn’t it the case that all friendships involved some amount of attraction? It was the engine of curiosity, the mystery that propelled any relationship forward.
item.dc:creator: ByAyşegül Savaş

item.title: What Dogs See When They Look at Us
item.description: A humane study of how artists have captured our canine companions becomes a meditation on sympathy, trust, and the virtues we have bred them to display.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “John of John,” “Body Double,” “The Rolling Stones,” and “Unvaccinated Under God.”

item.title: Looking Back at Lewis and Clark
item.description: The explorers’ crossing of the continent is America’s most famous camping trip. What was it all for?
item.dc:creator: ByCaleb Crain

item.title: The Fear Driving “Well, I’ll Let You Go” and “Othello”
item.description: A new Off Broadway play and Shakespeare’s tragedy hinge on a universal anxiety: How well do you know your partner?
item.dc:creator: ByEmily Nussbaum

item.title: “I Love Boosters,” Reviewed: A Socialist-Surrealist Shoplifting Fantasy
item.description: Boots Riley’s new film is an exuberantly inventive but overstretched comedy about the redistribution of luxury goods.
item.dc:creator: ByRichard Brody

item.title: “Ecologies of Perception”
item.description: “The blackfly breeds in streams & rivers / during the dog days of summer, / but cannot survive on sadness like the housefly.”
item.dc:creator: ByTerrance Hayes

item.title: “What I Saw”
item.description: “I laid my head on the place between my mother’s still-warm / arm and chest, closed my eyes, and cried.”
item.dc:creator: ByMatthew Dickman

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the June 1, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Wednesday, May 20, 2026
item.description: A beginner-friendly puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByCaitlin Reid
